personality_voice.synthesis: Books 1 and 2 score highest (22 each) because the films preserve Hagrid's core voice with minimal loss - dialect, warmth, impulsive loyalty, emotional transparency, and creature enthusiasm are all intact. The score degrades in books 3-6 as the films progressively strip away the emotional extremes: the drunken breakdown (PoA), the gruff scolding and wrongheaded anger (GoF), the defensive sulking and guilt-tripping (HBP), and the full giant-mission personality display (OotP). Book 5 is the most important book for Hagrid's personality range - it is where he is most fully realized - and the film loses most of what makes that true. DH (21) and HBP (20) show the films maintaining the surface voice while losing the pricklier, more insecure register. The pattern across all books is consistent: the films preserve the warm, loyal, emotionally open Hagrid but systematically remove the stubborn, self-deluding, occasionally wrongheaded, and socially anxious dimensions. A final score of 20 reflects a character whose dominant voice is faithfully rendered but whose full emotional range is consistently compressed.
personality_voice.weight_rationale: Books 1-2 establish the baseline and are well-served. Books 3-6 show progressive simplification. OotP is weighted most heavily as the richest book for this character; its losses pull the score down from the 21-22 range.

narrative_role_agency.synthesis: Books 1 and 2 score highest (17, 16) - Hagrid's narrative functions are largely intact in the early films. The score drops significantly from book 3 onward. The most serious agency failures are in OotP (13) and GoF (13): the giant mission - Hagrid's most significant act of independent agency in the entire series - is reduced to a two-minute summary, and his teaching role across GoF is almost entirely absent. HBP (14) removes his initiation of the Aragog burial (transferred to circumstance) and his role as plot-driving information source (Snape/Dumbledore disclosure). PoA (14) removes the Three Broomsticks information-conduit function. DH (15) preserves the escort role but omits the sidecar rescue. The consistent pattern is that the films retain Hagrid's reactive and emotional functions while cutting his proactive, plot-driving, and institutional ones. OotP is the most important book for this dimension and its failure is the most significant single drag on the final score.
narrative_role_agency.weight_rationale: OotP weighted most heavily - the giant mission is Hagrid's largest independent narrative contribution in the series. GoF and HBP also weighted significantly for the teaching role absence and burial initiation transfer respectively.

motivations_internal_conflict.synthesis: Books 1 and 2 score highest (13 each) - core motivations are intact and the internal conflict (discretion vs. indiscretion) is directly evidenced. The most significant failure is GoF (11), where the half-giant shame arc - the deepest internal conflict Hagrid has in any book - is entirely absent from the film. OotP (11) loses the Azkaban fear motivation that explains his passivity, and the self-delusion around Grawp is present but not developed. HBP (12) loses the insecurity about his teaching status and the Snape-disclosure panic. PoA (12) loses the Azkaban trauma motivation. DH (13) is largely faithful. The pattern: the films preserve Hagrid's external motivations (loyalty, creature love) but consistently remove the internal conflicts rooted in shame, fear, and social insecurity. GoF is the most important failure here.
motivations_internal_conflict.weight_rationale: GoF weighted most heavily for the complete absence of the shame arc. OotP and PoA weighted for the Azkaban fear omission, which affects understanding of his behavior in both books.

character_arc.synthesis: Books 1 and 2 score highest (13 each) - the vindication arc (CoS) and the consequence arc (PS) are both structurally preserved. GoF is the most serious failure (12 raw score, but the arc's defining movement - crisis and recovery - is entirely absent, making it effectively a flat non-arc in the film). OotP (10) loses the probation deterioration and the post-Sirius closing scene, leaving only the beginning and climax of a pressure arc. PoA (11) loses the sustained middle section. HBP (12) is compressed but faithful in shape. DH (12) is appropriate for a flat arc. The consistent failure is the middle of arcs - the films show the starting state and the climax but skip the development that gives arcs their weight. GoF is the most significant failure because the arc there is not merely compressed but structurally absent.
character_arc.weight_rationale: GoF weighted most heavily - the shame/recovery arc is the most complete personal arc Hagrid has in the series and its total absence is the largest single arc failure. OotP weighted for the probation deterioration loss.

key_relationships.synthesis: Scores are consistently in the 7-8 range across all books, reflecting a pattern of preserved emotional dynamics with reduced texture. The most significant losses are: the Three Broomsticks Harry-Hagrid scene (PoA), the post-Sirius Harry-Hagrid scene (OotP), the Madame Maxime half-giant revelation and estrangement (GoF), the trio reconciliation scene (HBP), and the birthday scene with the mokeskin purse (DH). The Harry-Hagrid relationship is the most important and it is consistently present in outline but loses its most emotionally specific scenes in books 3-7. The Dumbledore relationship is consistently preserved. The final score of 7 reflects relationships that are recognizable and emotionally legible but missing the scenes that give them their greatest depth.
key_relationships.weight_rationale: Harry-Hagrid relationship weighted most heavily across all books. GoF Madame Maxime relationship loss weighted for its structural significance to that book's arc.

complexity_nuance_lost_material.synthesis: This is the dimension with the most consistent and significant losses across all books. Books 1 and 2 score highest (11 each) - the losses are real but secondary. From book 3 onward the losses become structural: the Azkaban monologue (PoA), the drunken breakdown (PoA), the shame arc and wrongheaded anger (GoF), the sulking/guilt-tripping/disclosure panic (HBP), the giant mission complexity and probation diminishment (OotP). The pattern is clear and consistent: the films preserve the most emotionally legible aspects of Hagrid (warmth, grief, loyalty, physical presence) and remove the scenes that show his contradictions - the self-destruction, the shame, the stubbornness, the social anxiety, the fierce anger, the trauma. OotP is the most significant failure because it is the book where Hagrid is most fully realized as a complex character, and the film loses most of what makes that true. The final score of 9 reflects a character who is recognizable but systematically simplified toward the gentle-giant archetype.
complexity_nuance_lost_material.weight_rationale: OotP weighted most heavily as the richest book for this character's complexity. GoF and PoA weighted for the shame arc and Azkaban monologue losses respectively.

lost_or_transferred_material: Giant mission narrative (OotP) - reduced from multi-scene active protagonist role to two-minute summary; largest single agency loss in the series
Half-giant shame arc (GoF) - Skeeter exposé, withdrawal, depression, and recovery entirely absent; most significant internal conflict and character arc in the series
Azkaban trauma monologue (PoA) - the most psychologically specific passage in Hagrid's characterization across all books; absent from film
Drunken breakdown after first lesson (PoA) - absent; removes capacity for extreme emotional states and self-destructive grief
Three Broomsticks scene (PoA) - absent; removes information-conduit function and most intimate Harry-Hagrid moment in that book
Post-Sirius comfort scene (OotP) - absent; removes emotional closing of Harry-Hagrid relationship in that book
Probation deterioration arc (OotP) - largely absent; removes sustained portrayal of Hagrid diminished under institutional pressure
Snape/Dumbledore argument disclosure and panic (HBP) - absent; removes plot-driving information function and blurting/panic personality trait
Trio sulking/guilt-tripping/reconciliation scene (HBP) - absent; removes emotional dependency and mild manipulation dimensions
Karkaroff physical confrontation (GoF) - absent; removes fierce protectiveness of Dumbledore
Teaching role across GoF - almost entirely absent; skrewt and niffler lessons cut
Pumpkin charm scene (CoS) - absent; removes rule-bending and guilty relationship with expelled status
Lockhart criticism (CoS) - absent; removes irreverence toward distrusted authority figures
Aragog burial initiation transferred from Hagrid's letter to chance encounter (HBP) - meaningful agency reduction
Birthday scene with mokeskin purse (DH) - absent; removes thoughtful personal gift-giving as expression of relationship
Carrying scene emotional depth (DH) - compressed; reduces emotional apex of Harry-Hagrid relationship in the series
Firenze warning and Hagrid's dismissal (OotP) - absent; removes self-delusion beat of Grawp arc
Madame Maxime half-giant revelation and estrangement (GoF) - absent; simplifies most complex romantic relationship in the series
score_caps_applied: No hard caps applied. The PoA per-book score noted a cap at 75 due to transferred agency (Buckbeak rescue) and absent core narrative scene; this is factored into the narrative role/agency and complexity dimensions of the final score rather than applied as a mechanical cap to the total.
comparative.key_observations: Hagrid is a faithfully adapted character at the surface level - Robbie Coltrane's performance preserves the dialect, warmth, physical presence, emotional transparency, and loyalty that define the character across all seven films. The early films (PS, CoS) are the most faithful, with scores in the low-to-mid 80s. The adaptation quality degrades progressively from PoA onward as the films compress or eliminate the material that gives Hagrid psychological depth and narrative weight. The two most significant failures are the giant mission (OotP) and the half-giant shame arc (GoF): together they represent the loss of Hagrid's most extended display of independent agency and his most significant personal arc in the entire series. The consistent pattern across all books is that the films preserve Hagrid's reactive and emotional functions while cutting his proactive, plot-driving, and institutionally complex ones - and preserve his warmth and loyalty while removing his stubbornness, shame, social anxiety, and capacity for self-destruction. The result is a Hagrid who is recognizable and genuinely well-performed but systematically simplified toward the gentle-giant archetype, with the contradictions and vulnerabilities that make him more than that largely absent from the middle and later films. A final score of 73 reflects high surface fidelity undermined by consistent and meaningful loss of psychological complexity, narrative agency, and character arc development across the series.
